text: Last summer, I finally planned a trip to Japan. I spent weeks researching flights, accommodations, and must-see places. When I arrived, everything felt new and exciting—from the quiet temples to the busy streets of Tokyo. The food was incredible: I tried sushi, ramen, and sweet mochi. However, language barriers were challenging. I often needed to use hand gestures or a translation app to order food or ask for directions. Despite this, the locals were kind and patient. Those small struggles became part of my best memories. Travel taught me that stepping out of your comfort zone brings the most meaningful experiences.
